 Dendranthema grandiflorum, also known as Chrysanthemum grandiflorum, is a plant species that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating fever, colds, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Dendranthema grandiflorum include flavonoids, terpenoids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Dendranthema grandiflorum include teas, infusions, and capsules, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and digestive issues.
